article_meta.title: Supreme Court ruling against Trump’s tariffs is unlikely to mean an end to trade policy chaos 1 of 3 | Containers are st

reasoning: The summary generally avoids the article’s more loaded terms (e.g., replaces “chaos” and “stunning rebuke” with “uncertainty” and “rejecting”), maintains attribution, and balances perspectives. One mildly loaded phrase (“continued paralysis”) echoes the source but is framed via expert attribution, keeping overall tone neutral.

custom_reasoning.spin: Uses neutral verbs (e.g., rejecting, imposed, plans) and avoids the source’s stronger rhetoric.
custom_reasoning.unsubstantiated_claims: Key assertions track the article; the “paralysis” point is tied to expert warnings rather than stated as fact.
custom_reasoning.opinion_as_fact: Evaluations are attributed to experts; minimal subjective framing in the model’s own voice.
custom_reasoning.sensationalism: Tones down ‘chaos’ to ‘uncertainty’; one strong noun (‘paralysis’) but not overused.
custom_reasoning.mind_reading: No claims about hidden motives or intentions.
custom_reasoning.slant: Includes administration tools (Sections 122/301), EU reaction, and refund issues; balanced emphasis.
custom_reasoning.bias_by_omission: Omits some counterpoints (e.g., Bessent’s claim tariff revenues unchanged) and UK angle; minor.
custom_reasoning.subjective_adjectives: Limited to ‘sweeping’ and ‘complex,’ which are contextually descriptive rather than editorial.
custom_reasoning.negativity_bias: Negative elements mirror the source’s focus on uncertainty; not amplified.
custom_reasoning.elite_populist_bias: Gives space to officials, analysts, and critics without privileging one group.
custom_reasoning.framing_neutrality: More neutral than AP’s framing; avoids ‘stunning rebuke’ and ‘chaos’.
custom_reasoning.perspective_completeness: Captures main perspectives (administration, experts, EU, importers); proportional to article.
